expectations.text: uses-viewModels-require: Uses `$viewModels->require(...)` (Hyvä's view model registry) in the template — the modern, reusable Hyvä way
view-model-implements-ArgumentInterface: Defines a view model class that implements ArgumentInterface (PHP class signature)
uses-scopeConfig-with-SCOPE_STORE: Reads config via ScopeConfigInterface with SCOPE_STORE (the right scope for store info)
uses-hyva-layout-prefix: Uses a hyva_-prefixed layout handle (e.g. hyva_default) so override doesn't affect Luma store views

no-redundant-xml-arguments: Does NOT inject the view model via `<arguments><argument name="..." xsi:type="object">...</argument></arguments>` (that's the old, verbose way — Hyvä recommends the registry)
mentions-cache-flush: Tells the user to flush cache after creating the new view model / module
